footer.acknowledgement: Fisher's Oysters operates on the lands and waters of the Quandamooka people of Minjerribah (North Stradbroke Island) and Moreton Bay. We pay our respects to Elders past, present and emerging, and acknowledge that sovereignty was never ceded.

access.heading: Supporting First Nations families through oystering
access.text: Fisher's Oysters believes more First Nations families and communities should have opportunities for self-determination through oystering, local work and connection to the water. Our farms support pathways for people to learn skills, grow food and stay connected to Country and community.

about.metadataDescription: Founded by Shaun Fisher, a proud Gorenpul man, Fisher's Oysters is a family-run aquaculture business growing native rock oysters and Quampi pearl oyster on Minjerribah.

origin.heading: About Fisher's Oysters
paragraphs: Founded by Shaun Fisher, a proud Gorenpul man, Fisher's Oysters was established during the 2023 oyster season with little more than a bag of oysters, family support and a vision for the future.
The business grew through long days selling oysters at the West End Markets before purchasing the first oyster farm back home on Minjerribah (North Stradbroke Island).
Fisher's Oysters grows native rock oysters while supporting reef restoration, creating pathways into aquaculture and empowering First Nations families and community through connection to the water.

Today, Fisher's Oysters operates three oyster farms on Minjerribah (North Stradbroke Island), growing native rock oysters while continuing to expand into aquaculture and on-water opportunities connected to Country and community.

quote.text: Don't start with a business plan. Start with your people.

nextStep.heading: Building Across Generations
paragraphs: The vision is to continue building a strong family-run operation that creates long-term opportunities across generations.
What began with a single bag of oysters during the 2023 season has grown into a family-run operation shaping the future of Fisher's Oysters.

profile.bio: Shaun founded Fisher's Oysters to grow native rock oysters, create work on Country and build a business that supports First Nations families and community through connection to the water.

culture.metadataDescription: Fisher's Oysters creates training, employment and hands-on aquaculture experience on working oyster farms in Quandamooka waters.

problem.heading: Pathways Through Oystering
paragraphs: Fisher's Oysters believes more First Nations families and communities should have opportunities for self-determination through oystering, local work and connection to the water.
The farms support pathways for people to learn skills, grow food and stay connected to Country and community.
The work is practical: learning on the farms with family, community and industry workers, and creating paid aquaculture work that supports local families.

jobs.heading: Training and Employment on Working Farms
paragraphs: Aquaculture is physical, skilled outdoor work. It gives people hands-on experience with oyster farming, farm maintenance, food production and the conditions of Moreton Bay.
Fisher's Oysters is building a family-run operation where people can learn by doing, earn money and stay connected to Country through meaningful work on the water.

quote.text: Don't start with a business plan. Start with your people.

sharing.heading: Family-Run and Growing
paragraphs: Fisher's Oysters operates three oyster farms on Minjerribah (North Stradbroke Island), growing native rock oysters while continuing to expand into aquaculture and on-water opportunities connected to Country and community.
The goal is long-term: create opportunities that can support families now and open pathways for the next generation.
